content.assessment: This proposal requires reconstructing municipal legal standing that was removed by the 2011-2013 centralization, which terminated schools as independent legal entities and transferred maintainer authority to a state agency (F-live-legal_and_governance-01). Any reciprocal contract model presumes municipalities have some bargaining power or reciprocal obligation capacity; current governance places school-director appointment with the Minister and gives district councils only advisory input (F-live-legal_and_governance-02), meaning municipalities lack the legal authority to negotiate as counterparties in the French sense. The existing precedent for non-state actors assuming maintainer-type responsibility is the church-transfer mechanism, which requires majority parental support and has seen unclear uptake for depopulating villages (F-live-legal_and_governance-06), and the minority self-government transfer mechanism, which has similarly limited uptake (F-live-legal_and_governance-07). Both indicate that alternative-maintainer arrangements exist in Hungarian law but remain underused, suggesting that a new state-municipality contract instrument would need entirely new statutory grounding rather than adaptation of an existing mechanism.
opportunities: A new contractual instrument could be legislated as part of broader reform, using the church/minority transfer models as partial legal precedent for shared or conditional maintainer authority
strengths: Existing legal precedents for non-state maintainer transfer (church, minority self-government) show the legal system already accommodates alternative maintainer arrangements, offering some template mechanics (F-live-legal_and_governance-06, F-live-legal_and_governance-07)
threats: Without new legislation restoring some municipal legal standing, the contract mechanism has no clear legal object to attach to, risking non-enforceability from inception
weaknesses: Municipalities currently possess no independent legal maintainer status since 2013 centralization, undermining their capacity to be genuine reciprocal contracting parties (F-live-legal_and_governance-01)

District councils have only advisory input and directors are ministerially appointed, meaning local governance actors lack the authority a reciprocal contract would presume they hold (F-live-legal_and_governance-02)

Existing alternative-maintainer transfer mechanisms (church, minority self-government) show limited uptake, suggesting weak institutional appetite or capacity for non-standard maintainer arrangements even where legally possible (F-live-legal_and_governance-06, F-live-legal_and_governance-07)
